untranslatable_reason: The double top pattern requires explicit peak detection, valley depth measurement (10-20% decline), and inter-peak spacing validation. These require multi-bar pattern recognition and relative price comparisons across variable lookback windows that cannot be expressed using single-bar indicator conditions and the allowed operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is). Specifically: (1) identifying 'twin peaks at near same price level' requires peak-finding logic; (2) measuring 'valley declining 10-20%' requires comparing three specific swing points; (3) validating 'widely spaced' peaks requires measuring bar distance between peaks. No combination of ALLOWED_INDICATORS can encode this pattern structure.
translation_notes: This is a classic candlestick/bar pattern from Bulkowski that fundamentally requires pattern-matching logic beyond point-in-time indicator evaluation. A proper backtest implementation would need: (1) A swing high/low detector to identify the two peaks and intervening valley; (2) Logic to confirm peaks are within 2-3% of each other; (3) Logic to measure valley decline as percentage of first peak; (4) Logic to confirm spacing is 'wide' (typically 10+ bars); (5) Break of valley floor as entry trigger. Consider using a dedicated pattern recognition library or implementing custom pattern logic outside the indicator-based rule engine.
